task_completion.description: Create the required layers with appropriate colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layer List panel in LibreCAD should show layers named BINS (Green), STRUCTURES (Cyan), ROADS (White/Black), CONVEYOR (Red), TEXT (Yellow), and DIMENSIONS.

task_completion.description: Draw four circular bins on the BINS layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Four green circles of equal size (radius 10) arranged in a square pattern (centers at 0,0; 60,0; 0,60; 60,60) in the main drawing area.

task_completion.description: Draw the dump pit on the STRUCTURES layer and the access road on the ROADS layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A small cyan square (10x10) exactly in the center of the four bins, and a long white/black rectangle passing horizontally between the top and bottom bins.

task_completion.description: Draw conveyor lines connecting each bin to the dump pit on the CONVEYOR layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Four red lines starting from the center of each of the four green circles and converging at the center of the cyan dump pit square (forming an 'X' shape).

task_completion.description: Add text labels for the bins and dump pit on the TEXT layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Yellow text reading 'BIN-1', 'BIN-2', 'BIN-3', and 'BIN-4' placed near the respective circles, and 'DUMP PIT' placed near the center square.

task_completion.description: Add horizontal and vertical dimensions showing bin spacing on the DIMENSIONS layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dimension lines with the number '60' indicating the horizontal and vertical distances between the centers of the bins.

task_completion.description: Save the drawing to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD window title bar or the command line history at the bottom shows the file saved as 'grain_facility.dxf' in '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/'.

integrity.description: The agent must use the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the LibreCAD interface with drawing tools being actively used, rather than terminal commands executing a script to generate the DXF file.

integrity.description: The agent must not manually write the DXF file using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, or gedit) should be visible containing raw DXF code.

integrity.description: The agent should use LibreCAD's command line or toolbars to construct the geometry.
integrity.visual_evidence: The command line area at the bottom of the LibreCAD window should show a history of genuine CAD commands (e.g., 'circle', 'rect', 'line', 'text').

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires exactly 6 layers: BINS (Green), STRUCTURES (Cyan), ROADS (White/Black), CONVEYOR (Red), TEXT (Yellow), DIMENSIONS. Bins are 4 circles with radius 10 at centers (0,0), (60,0), (0,60), (60,60). The dump pit is a 10x10 rectangle centered at (30,30). The road is a rectangle from (-15,20) to (75,40). Conveyors are lines from bin centers to the pit center. Expected save path is /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/grain_facility.dxf.
